old_claim.note: Printed Patent Specifications provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Patent Office retained submitted models, specifications, drawings, and records, turning application artifacts into a public institutional collection.

source_shape.source_locator: Smithsonian says the Patent Office became keeper of a huge model collection and that 1840 restoration recovered models and specifications lost in the 1836 fire.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies Patent Office custody and restoration of models and specifications as the collection mechanism.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing printed specifications with public archives because the collection was formed by institutional retention of application artifacts rather than publication alone.

ontology_before: Patent Model Collections depended on printed patent specifications, overemphasizing publication instead of retained physical and documentary records.
ontology_after: Patent Model Collections depends on public_archives for Patent Office custody of models, specifications, drawings, and records.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: patent_model_collections now depends on public_archives and no longer directly on printed_patent_specifications.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to printed patent documents without retained physical models.
A source showed patent model collections emerged from publication rather than Patent Office custody.
The public_archives node were narrowed away from official institutional record repositories.
short_reason: The model collection was an archive of retained application artifacts, not a printed-specification system.
